daily_fact: Time Out reported New Yorkers could soon install window-mounted solar panels, potentially expanding clean-energy access for apartment residents across New York City.
topline_md: New York City faces major policy and public-safety shifts, from possible cuts to foster-care prevention, lawsuits over trans healthcare records, and expanded NYPD overtime, to housing, transit, and data-center moves reshaping development.

items.title: NYC may cut foster care prevention services
items.why_it_matters: Cuts could push more vulnerable families into crisis and increase child removals across the city.

items.title: NYPD plans 12-hour shifts, $100 million overtime summer
items.why_it_matters: The policing plan affects city spending, officer workload, and security at major summer events.

items.title: State weighs moratorium on new data centers
items.why_it_matters: A pause could reshape local energy use, land development, and future tech investment in New York.

items.title: City advances 34th Street busway in Midtown
items.why_it_matters: The project could speed buses, change traffic patterns, and affect daily commutes through Midtown.

items.title: NYC to install traffic sensors citywide
items.why_it_matters: The sensors may influence street design, enforcement, and privacy for pedestrians, cyclists, and drivers.

items.title: Trans New Yorkers sue over federal healthcare records access
items.why_it_matters: The case could determine privacy protections for sensitive medical data used by New Yorkers.

items.title: Housing agency targets 99-unit projects under tax break
items.why_it_matters: The policy could shape what housing gets built and how affordable future apartments are.

items.title: Bill would remove 'sell by' dates in New York
items.why_it_matters: The proposal could change grocery labeling, reduce food waste, and affect how residents judge freshness.

items.title: NYC launches 2-K application process
items.why_it_matters: Families can now apply for free early childhood seats, affecting childcare access and school planning citywide.

items.title: NYC wins state downtown revitalization funding
items.why_it_matters: New state investment could support neighborhood improvements, jobs, and local business activity in the winning area.

items.title: DOT expands pilot to cut delivery truck miles
items.why_it_matters: Reducing truck travel may ease congestion, improve air quality, and affect deliveries in busy neighborhoods.

items.title: Bronx major fires have doubled, alarming residents
items.why_it_matters: Rising major fires threaten safety, housing stability, and emergency response capacity in Bronx neighborhoods.

items.title: Best free cultural events in NYC this summer
items.why_it_matters: Residents can access low-cost arts and community activities across the city during summer.

items.title: Seaport announces free outdoor summer movie lineup
items.why_it_matters: The series offers families and neighbors free evening entertainment in Lower Manhattan.

items.title: Interactive map helps kids find free summer activities
items.why_it_matters: Parents can more easily locate free local outings and programs for children citywide.

items.title: New ferry reaches Jersey Shore from NYC quickly
items.why_it_matters: The route expands summer travel options for New Yorkers without cars.
